fact: PHP/Yii2 'is X an ActiveRecord/Module/Migration?' detection MUST walk the structural extends chain via index.symbols.find(name=last-segment), not regex on cls.source. Aliased imports (User extends BaseUser via use foo\bar\User as BaseUser) and transitive bases are common in real Yii2 codebases (tgm-panel: dektrium/yii2-user pattern).
recommendation: Implement isXHierarchy() helpers with: visited Set cycle protection, depth cap of 5, last-namespace-segment match against AR_ROOT_NAMES, and a source-regex fallback for stale pre-v2.0.0 indexes. Use this for ActiveRecord, Module, console Controller, Migration detection.

fact: searchPatterns iterates index.symbols, not files. Files containing only top-level statements (return [...] config arrays, define() entry points like web/index.php) produce ZERO hits even when the pattern would match perfectly against the raw source.
recommendation: For module-level patterns (yii-debug-mode-prod, yii-cookie-no-validation, yii-config-hardcoded-secret, yii-translate-in-loop), implement a parallel file-level scan path (see runFileLevelChecks() in php-tools.ts). Expose a FILE_LEVEL_PATTERNS Set so patterns can opt in.

fact: Bumping EXTRACTOR_VERSIONS.<lang> from 1.0.0 to 2.0.0 forces a full reindex of that language's files, but downstream tools must remain robust to legacy indexes (extends/implements/meta absent). Add a source-regex fallback in every consumer, and keep all extractor metadata fields OPTIONAL on CodeSymbol.
recommendation: On extractor schema changes: bump version, add fallback paths in consumers, document the bump in commit body. Existing users get auto-reindex on first MCP call after upgrade.

fact: Inferring nested config-array section ('modules.X.components.Y' vs 'container.singletons') from a string prefix CANNOT use regex with non-greedy [\s\S]*?. Brackets close before the regex realizes — non-greedy matches the FIRST encountered keyword without honoring depth.
recommendation: Use a forward-walking bracket-balanced parser with a stack of {key, depth} frames. Skip string literals and comments before the bracket counter. See inferConfigSection() in php-tools.ts for the canonical implementation.

fact: Audit checks that look for guard tokens (e.g. ALGORITHM=INPLACE, LOCK=NONE, AllowDynamicProperties) in raw file content silently fail when developers MENTION the token in comments. Common case: TODO comment 'add ALGORITHM=INPLACE here' silences the warning meant to flag the missing hint.
recommendation: Strip comments BEFORE searching for guard tokens. stripPhpComments() in yii-migrations-tools.ts is the reference implementation: preserves newlines (so line numbers stay aligned), handles //, #, /* */ blocks.

fact: The canonical PHP scan exclusion regex in this repo is /(^|\/)(?:vendor|node_modules|runtime|tests\/_data)(\/|$)/. Used in 6 of 10 new PHP tools. The 4 that omit it (yii-modules, yii-migrations, yii-console, phpstan-baseline) inherit indexer-level exclusions because they scan index.symbols/index.files exclusively.
recommendation: Always replicate this VENDOR_RE shape in any new file-reading PHP tool. Consider extracting to a shared util in src/utils/php-paths.ts (currently TODO'd as a NIT in zuvo:review).
